title: Temporarily Isolating Rows in a Separate Entity
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
Temporarily Isolating Rows in a Separate Entity
text: Temporarily Isolating Rows in a Separate Entity Temporarily storing related data from several entities into a single entity for subsequent processing can result in a significant performance improvement. This is case if the following conditions are true: Data is needed from more than one entity The entities involved consist of a relatively large number of rows. The data is accessed repeatedly. This situation typically occurs in transactions with intensive processing of a large number of data. The data needed is isolated at the beginning of the transaction and removed when the transaction is finished. The performance gain is obtained because the number of rows to be scanned in the course of the transaction are reduced and table joining is eliminated. Figure 1.
